world.title: Bop Foundations
world.description: A slower, beginner-first path through Bop fundamentals with extra practice before each new concept.
world.story_intro: Take your time, practice each idea, and build confidence before moving on.

levels.title: Step by Step
levels.description: Move to the goal using simple right moves.
levels.tutorial:
# Welcome to bopcode!

You're going to learn to code by solving puzzles. You control a **bot** on a grid by writing instructions.

## Your first command: `move()`

The `move()` function moves your bot one tile in a direction:

```
move("right")
```

Each call moves the bot **one step**. To move further, use `move()` multiple times.

## Your mission

The green tile is the **goal**. Write enough `move("right")` calls to get there!

---

Click the **Puzzle** tab at the top to see the grid and start solving!

levels.title: Longer Walk
levels.description: Reach a farther goal with clean, consistent movement.
levels.hint: Keep moving right until the bot stands on the goal tile.
levels.tutorial:
# Practice: More Movement

Same idea as last time, just a longer path. This is a good chance to get comfortable writing `move()` calls.

## How your code runs

Your instructions run **top to bottom**, one at a time:

```
move("right")   // runs first
move("right")   // runs second
move("right")   // runs third
```

The bot follows each instruction in order.

## Your mission

The goal is further away this time. Keep moving right until you reach it.

levels.title: Turn the Corner
levels.description: Use a mix of directions to reach the goal.
levels.hint: Move right first, then down to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Moving in All Directions

So far you've only moved right. But the grid is 2D, so you can move in **four directions**:

```
move("right")
move("left")
move("up")
move("down")
```

## Walls

The dark tiles are **walls**. You can't walk through them. If you try, your program will show a warning.

## Your mission

The goal isn't in a straight line any more. You'll need to combine directions to get there.

levels.title: Movement Checkpoint
levels.description: Navigate a short route with walls and one final turn.
levels.hint: Find the gap in the wall row, then route to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint: Movement

Time to put your movement skills together. This level has walls and a turn, so you'll need to plan your route before writing code.

## Tips

- Look at the grid and trace a path from the bot to the goal
- Figure out which directions you need, and in what order
- Remember: each `move()` is one step

There's nothing new here. If you can solve this, you're ready for the next concept!

levels.title: First Gem Pickup
levels.description: Collect the gem and then reach the goal.
levels.hint: Stand on the gem tile before calling grab().
levels.tutorial:
# Collecting Gems

Some tiles have **gems** on them. You can pick them up using the `grab()` function.

## How `grab()` works

When your bot is standing on a tile with a gem, call:

```
grab()
```

This picks up the gem. If there's no gem on the tile, `grab()` will cause an error, so only use it when you're on a gem!

## Your mission

Move to the gem, grab it, then continue to the goal.

levels.title: Two Gems in a Row
levels.description: Collect both gems, then finish at the goal.
levels.hint: Move and grab in sequence so no gem is missed.
levels.tutorial:
# Multiple Gems

This time there are two gems to collect. Your code still runs top to bottom, so plan the full path in advance.

## Sequencing

Think about the order of actions:

```
move("right")   // walk to gem
move("right")
grab()           // pick it up
move("right")   // keep going
```

You need to be standing on the gem tile before calling `grab()`.

## Your mission

Collect both gems and reach the goal.

levels.title: Gem Practice Checkpoint
levels.description: A longer line with empty spaces between gems.
levels.hint: Do not call grab() on empty tiles.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint: Gems

A longer path with gems spaced out along it. Be careful not to call `grab()` on a tile that doesn't have a gem.

## Planning ahead

It helps to look at the grid and note exactly which tiles have gems. Then write your `move()` and `grab()` calls in the right order.

Once you've got this, we'll introduce a way to avoid writing so many lines of code!

levels.title: Your First repeat Loop
levels.description: Use repeat to avoid writing the same line many times.
levels.hint: Increase the repeat count until the bot reaches the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Loops: `repeat`

Writing `move("right")` eight times works, but there's a better way.

## The `repeat` block

`repeat` runs the same code multiple times:

```
repeat 3 {
    move("right")
}
```

This is exactly the same as writing `move("right")` three times, but much shorter.

## How it works

- `repeat N { ... }` runs the code inside the braces **N times**
- N can be any whole number
- The code inside the `{ }` can be any instructions

## Your mission

The starter code already has a `repeat` block but the count is too low. Adjust the number so the bot reaches the goal.

levels.title: repeat and grab
levels.description: Apply a repeating move pattern while collecting gems.
levels.hint: Use a 2-step loop body: move right, grab if gem_here(), move right. Repeat that pattern 4 times.
levels.tutorial:
# Multiple Actions in a Loop

A `repeat` block can contain **more than one instruction**:

```
repeat 3 {
    move("right")
    grab()
}
```

This moves right **and** grabs, three times in a row. The bot does both actions in order, then goes back to the top of the loop.

## Think in patterns

Look at the grid. Is there a repeating pattern? If the bot needs to do the same sequence of actions over and over, that's a perfect loop body.

## Your mission

Gems are spread along the path. Find the pattern and write a loop that handles it.

levels.title: Repeat Checkpoint
levels.description: Use loops and path planning to collect gems in a small route.
levels.hint: Go right to the center opening, drop down to the gem row, clear left gem first, then sweep right through the remaining gems to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint: Loops

This level combines movement, gems, and loops in a 2D map. You might need more than one `repeat` block if the path changes direction.

## Multiple loops

You can use several `repeat` blocks in sequence:

```
repeat 5 {
    move("right")
}
repeat 3 {
    move("down")
}
```

The first loop finishes completely before the second one starts.

## Your mission

Navigate the route, collect all the gems, and reach the goal. Use loops to keep your code short.

levels.title: Only Grab When Needed
levels.description: Use if gem_here() so grab() only runs on gem tiles.
levels.hint: Conditionals are useful when not every tile needs the same action.
levels.tutorial:
# Conditionals: `if`

Sometimes you only want to do something when a certain condition is true. That's what `if` is for.

## The `if` statement

```
if gem_here() {
    grab()
}
```

This grabs **only** if there's a gem on the current tile. If there's no gem, it does nothing and moves on.

## Sensors

Your bot has built-in sensors that return `true` or `false`:

| Sensor | Returns `true` when... |
|---|---|
| `gem_here()` | There's a gem on your current tile |
| `path_ahead()` | The tile ahead is walkable |
| `wall_ahead()` | The tile ahead is a wall |

## Combining `if` with loops

You can put `if` inside a loop to check on every step:

```
repeat 5 {
    move("right")
    if gem_here() {
        grab()
    }
}
```

## Your mission

Gems are scattered along the path but not on every tile. Use `if gem_here()` to grab safely.

levels.title: if Practice Lap
levels.description: Collect all gems in a small map with branches.
levels.hint: Move right in a loop, but only grab when there is a gem on the current tile.
levels.tutorial:
# Practice: Conditionals

A longer corridor with gems in unpredictable positions. You don't want to hardcode where each gem is.

The pattern from last time works well here:

```
repeat N {
    move("right")
    if gem_here() {
        grab()
    }
}
```

This is a really common pattern in programming: loop through something, and act only when a condition is met.

## Your mission

Collect every gem on the way to the goal. Let `if` do the thinking for you.

levels.title: while Until the Wall
levels.description: Use while path_ahead() to move until blocked.
levels.hint: Use move("forward") inside the while loop to keep moving right until you hit the wall, then turn right and repeat.
levels.tutorial:
# While Loops

With `repeat`, you need to know exactly how many times to loop. But sometimes you don't know the count in advance. That's where `while` comes in.

## The `while` loop

A `while` loop keeps running **as long as its condition is true**:

```
while path_ahead() {
    move("right")
}
```

This moves right until there's no more open path ahead. The bot checks the condition before each step.

## `while` vs `repeat`

- Use `repeat N` when you know the exact count
- Use `while condition` when you want to keep going until something changes

## Your mission

Use `while` to walk until you're blocked, then change direction and keep going.

levels.title: while + if Together
levels.description: Combine a loop and a conditional to collect gems on the way.
levels.hint: Move first, then check for a gem on the current tile.
levels.tutorial:
# Combining `while` + `if`

Now let's put `while` and `if` together. This is one of the most useful patterns in programming.

```
while path_ahead() {
    move("right")
    if gem_here() {
        grab()
    }
}
```

Each time around the loop: move one step, then check for a gem. This repeats until the path ends.

## Why this matters

This pattern shows up everywhere in real code: "keep processing items, and handle each one based on what it is." You're already thinking like a programmer.

## Your mission

Walk to the goal and collect every gem along the way.

levels.title: Checkpoint: Gentle Maze
levels.description: A calm maze that reinforces planning, loops, and safe grabs.
levels.hint: Take the center opening to get the left gem first, return to the top lane, move to the right opening for the second gem, then climb back to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint: Putting It Together

This is a small maze that uses everything you've learned so far: movement in multiple directions, `grab()`, loops, and conditionals.

## Your toolbox so far

| Tool | What it does |
|---|---|
| `move(dir)` | Move one tile in a direction |
| `grab()` | Pick up a gem |
| `repeat N { }` | Run code N times |
| `while cond { }` | Loop while condition is true |
| `if cond { }` | Run code only if condition is true |

## Tips

Break the maze into sections. Solve each section one at a time, then combine them.

## Your mission

Navigate the maze, collect all gems, and reach the goal.

levels.title: Bop Foundations Finale
levels.description: Final beginner challenge: collect gems and finish with clean structure.
levels.hint: Section it: grab the left gem, route through the middle bridge to the top-right lane, drop to the bottom-right gem, then return up and finish at the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Bop Foundations Finale

This is the biggest level yet. There's no single right answer, so use whatever combination of tools feels right.

## Looking back

You started by writing `move("right")` one step at a time. Now you can write loops that adapt to the grid and conditionals that make decisions for you. These are the same fundamental concepts used in every programming language.

## Your mission

Collect all the gems and reach the goal. Good luck!
